problem: The resolveTicket() function in src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts only fetches active tickets from GET /api/tickets (no query params). It never requests archived tickets (queryParams.archived = 'true'). Commands that route through resolveTicket (get, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, bundle, comments list, comments post) therefore fail with 'not found' for archived ticket references, even though the server can return archived ticket data.

files.why: Primary fix target. Contains resolveTicket() (line 83-127) which calls hxFetch(config, '/tickets', { basePath: '/api' }) at line 89 without archived query param. Also contains matchTicket() (line 44-77), the pure matching function that works on whatever items it receives. The ambiguity check at lines 102-121 also only considers the single active-tickets list.

Existing test file with tests for matchTicket() and extractTicketRef(). No tests for resolveTicket() itself (it requires mocking hxFetch). New tests for archived resolution and cross-set ambiguity detection must be added here.

hxFetch() utility used by resolveTicket(). Already supports queryParams option (line 40-49). The archived ticket API call pattern is: hxFetch(config, '/tickets', { basePath: '/api', queryParams: { archived: 'true' } }). This is the same pattern used by list.ts and latest.ts.

Command router for tickets subcommands. Lines 66, 85, 96, 107, 118, 129, 140 call resolveTicket() for get, update-description, rerun, continue, artifacts, artifact, and bundle commands respectively. These are all affected by the resolver's active-only limitation. No changes expected here since the fix is in the shared resolver.
Command router for comments subcommands. Lines 32 and 43 call resolveTicket() for list and post commands respectively. Affected by the same resolver limitation.

Boundary file: shows the established API pattern for fetching archived tickets. Line 43-44: if (hasFlag(args, '--archived')) { queryParams.archived = 'true'; }. This command does NOT use resolveTicket and must remain unchanged.
Boundary file: also shows the --archived flag pattern at line 24-25. Does NOT use resolveTicket and must remain unchanged.

Downstream command after resolution. Line 47 shows the direct ticket endpoint: hxFetch(config, `/tickets/${ticketId}`, { basePath: '/api' }). The TicketDetail type (line 6-22) includes isArchived: boolean. The server already returns archived ticket details by ID.
Defines HxConfig type (line 13-20) used as parameter to resolveTicket(). Contains orgName (used in error messages at lines 119, 124 of resolve-ticket.ts).
Utility module for flag parsing. Used by resolve-ticket.ts (getFlag at line 3). No changes expected.
Build and quality gate scripts: build (tsc), typecheck (tsc --noEmit), test (tsc && node --test dist/**/*.test.js). Zero runtime dependencies. Node >=18.
TypeScript config: strict mode, ES2022 target, Node16 modules. Compilation to dist/.

facts: resolveTicket() (src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:83-127) fetches only from GET /api/tickets with no query params, returning only active tickets.
The API supports an 'archived=true' query parameter on GET /api/tickets, as demonstrated by list.ts:43-44 and latest.ts:24-25.
hxFetch() already supports queryParams option (src/lib/http.ts:40-49), so no http-layer changes are needed.
matchTicket() (src/lib/resolve-ticket.ts:44-77) is a pure function operating on any items array. It supports three ref forms: exact internal ID, case-insensitive short ID, and numeric ticket number.

resolveTicket() is called from 9 command sites: tickets get (line 66), update-description (85), rerun (96), continue (107), artifacts (118), artifact (129), bundle (140) in src/tickets/index.ts, and comments list (32) and post (43) in src/comments/index.ts.
The ambiguity detection logic in resolveTicket() (lines 102-121) re-scans the same active-only items array, meaning it cannot detect numeric ambiguity between an active ticket and an archived ticket.
tickets list and tickets latest do NOT use resolveTicket() — they call hxFetch('/tickets', ...) directly with their own query params. They must remain unaffected.
tickets create does NOT use resolveTicket() — it creates new tickets.
The TicketDetail type in get.ts includes 'isArchived: boolean' (line 22), confirming the server already models archived state.
Existing tests cover matchTicket() and extractTicketRef() only — there are no integration-level tests for resolveTicket() itself.
The test runner is Node's built-in test runner (node --test). Tests require tsc compilation first.
The repo has zero runtime dependencies (devDependencies only: @types/node, typescript). No test mocking library is available; mocks must use node:test's built-in mock support.
unknowns: Whether GET /api/tickets?archived=true returns ONLY archived tickets or BOTH active and archived tickets. list.ts uses it as a toggle, which suggests it returns only archived tickets when set to 'true'.
Whether the API supports a single call to get both active and archived tickets simultaneously, or if two separate calls are required.

The exact server-side behavior when downstream endpoints (rerun, continue, etc.) are called with an archived ticket ID — ticket says these should surface real server errors, but the actual error shapes are unknown.
Whether there is a rate limit or performance concern with making two parallel API calls (active + archived) during resolution.
Whether node:test's mock.method or mock.fn is available in the project's minimum Node 18 target for mocking hxFetch in resolveTicket() tests.
